## [00:00 - 01:07]

Comparing yourself to others. Look if you want to do that this is a common problem. I'm better off than Jack. I'm better off than Jill. I'm better off than my uncle. I'm doing good. Comparing yourself to others is crazy. 76% of all working Americans live paycheck to paycheck. We're not talking rich. We're talking, I can't get by. And one of the problems here is this middle-class phenomenon that happens. I know people in America that compare themselves to people in India and have never been to India probably couldn't find it on the globe. Never ever compare yourself to somebody else's financial condition. There's always somebody worse off and there's always somebody better off. There's a no-win situation. You're better off than the homeless person and you're way worse off than Warren Buffett. Okay? Never justify your situation. I'm better off because that doesn't matter. Another person's finances good, bad, and different will never pay your bills, won't fund your retirement and will not provide you with peace of mind, won't provide you with what you're gonna need to get through hard times. Don't compare your finances to someone else's. It's a mistake. It'll keep you from wealth.
